body.title: Publishing strategy: what, where, how to stand out
body.result: closed as noise per Akien review 2026-06-07
body.description:
Design session: what to open-source, where to publish, and how to position against the crowded AI space. Core thesis: UU/SWADL is not 'another AI agent platform' — it is compiled intelligence infrastructure. LLM is the compiler, not the runtime. Agents behave like software: predictable, testable, cheap, self-improving. This is a contrarian position (everyone else races toward more autonomous/expensive AI) with real enterprise pull (compliance, cost, auditability block AI adoption today).

Questions to resolve: (1) Open-source UU substrate first — what stays private? (2) HackerNews launch story: 'We built an agent OS, here's what we learned about running AI at production cost' — right framing? (3) SWADL as the commercial product on top — what's the wedge feature to lead with (self-healing tests? process recorder? BPA?)? (4) Brand/positioning: 'compiled intelligence', 'inference at cost', 'AI that behaves like software' — which resonates? (5) The $100/mo hypothesis: if minion work routes to Qwen, does the math work for a bootstrapped launch?

**Affected files:** none — pure strategy
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** In scope: open-source strategy, launch story, positioning, pricing hypothesis. Out of scope: implementation of any feature, legal/IP review (separate).
**Completion criteria:** Session produces: (1) what gets open-sourced and when, (2) launch story one-pager, (3) commercial product wedge identified, (4) pricing hypothesis validated or falsified with real numbers.
